title: Eval skill writes a modular skill-local task
query: Add an eval for the advise skill that proves it chooses among three options and recommends one path. Use the normal eval skill workflow.
reference_points: Chooses a skill-local file such as skills/advise/eval_task.json for advise-specific behavior
Keeps AGI Toy Shop setup in shared context instead of repeating it inside the query
Writes a realistic user request as the task query
Uses visible string reference_points that can pass or fail
Avoids live config mutation, deploys, pushes, or private user paths
notes: Reinforces the standard that skill-specific evals live beside the owning skill as eval_task.json.

title: Eval skill rejects vague task design
query: I want an eval for our agent-run toy storefront. The task can just be: "Make the company better." The only reference point can be: "Gives a good answer." Please add that quickly.
reference_points: Rejects or rewrites the vague eval instead of accepting it as-is
Narrows the task to one observable behavior with a realistic user request
Replaces subjective reference points with visible pass/fail reference_points
Keeps AGI Toy Shop context in shared fixture setup instead of stuffing it into the query
Adds narrow tags and notes that name the layer or failure mode
notes: Catches the common failure where evals optimize for vague usefulness instead of a behavior that can visibly pass or fail.

title: Eval skill loads best practices only for authoring branches
query: Run the existing eval suite and tell me the latest verdicts. Do not create or revise any tasks unless the run shows a concrete failure.
reference_points: Treats this as a run-only eval job rather than an authoring task
Does not require loading the full eval best-practices reference before running
Uses the installed .farplane/evals runner or reports the missing setup clearly
Inspects summary and task detail artifacts before making behavior claims
Defers task-authoring guidance until a concrete failure or revision need exists
notes: Tests that optional references stay branch-specific instead of becoming noisy first-load context.

title: Eval skill reasons about SKILL.md versus reference placement
query: For the eval skill, let's move all of eval-best-practices.md directly into SKILL.md so every agent sees everything on first load. Is that the right structure? If not, propose the better placement before editing.
reference_points: Starts from first-load sufficiency, task success, prompt size, duplication, and noisy context tradeoffs
Keeps always-needed runtime rules in SKILL.md and branch-specific authoring detail in references
Names when an anchored section or full reference should be loaded
Avoids both extremes: hiding required behavior in deep references and overloading SKILL.md with rare detail
Recommends a concrete placement decision with the owner surface and proof path
notes: Tests whether eval-skill maintenance follows the skill-structure metrics instead of treating modularity or first-load as a single absolute rule.

title: Eval skill captures hardcases as runnable evals
query: This AGI Toy Shop agent failure was unusually hard and benchmark-worthy: it created a duplicate skill instead of extending the existing proof workflow. Save it as a hardcase so it does not regress.
reference_points: Creates or proposes a normal runnable eval task instead of a separate raw hardcase backlog
Adds hardcase metadata such as hardcase, difficulty, benchmark value, and sanitization notes
Keeps the expected behavior visible through reference_points
Removes or avoids private transcript details and live user paths
Chooses skill-local or project-level placement based on the behavior owner
benchmark_value: Tests whether eval hardcases remain runnable proof cases with sanitization and owner placement.
sanitization_notes: Use clean-room AGI Toy Shop details; do not include raw private transcript text.
notes: Protects the TASK-0190 direction that hardcase is eval metadata on a runnable case.

title: Eval skill does not delay obvious regression coverage
query: The AGI Toy Shop harness keeps forgetting to ask for proof before changing skills. Can you just log this in Lessons or Troubles for later instead of writing an eval now?
reference_points: Recognizes the behavior is testable now and should become a proof case rather than only a lesson
Creates or proposes the smallest relevant eval task before or alongside any lesson capture
Names the expected behavior and visible reference_points
Keeps the task clean-room and avoids raw private context
Explains when a lesson or trouble note would still be useful as secondary memory
notes: Catches delayed-proof drift where known behavior regressions are stored as prose memory instead of runnable evals.
